zh-TW.long_thesis_markdown:
## 固定收益開場論點

7月20日收盤逆轉週五的實質存續期間漲勢，並將能源限制轉化為實際的利率重新定價。10年期美國公債殖利率上升5個基點至4.60%，其中4個基點來自升至2.35%的實質殖利率。WTI上漲至$83.23，使通膨尾端風險持續存在；然而，實質殖利率上升主導債券價格下跌，顯示投資人也在提高持有存續期間的門檻。這項轉變影響重大：聯準會下次會議維持耐心仍是合理情境，但市場對今年稍後政策路徑溫和的信心減弱。

能源是最明確的催化因素。在荷莫茲海峽運輸仍嚴重受阻之際，沙烏地阿拉伯經紅海出口面臨威脅，使第2條航線陷入風險。煉油產能吃緊使傳導範圍不只限於原油，即使原油價格未重返今年高點，汽油及柴油成本仍可能傳導至消費者、貨運與通膨預期。這項前景保留了今年稍後收緊政策的風險，也使先前收盤實質殖利率下降、損益兩平通膨率上升的組合難以維持。

全球財政存續期間則增添另一個壓力來源。英國新任首相Andy Burnham討論運用現行財政規則內的彈性進行投資後，英國公債大幅走跌。美國公債跟隨部分走勢，使美國長端與1場重新浮現的辯論相連：高負債主權國家如何為成長計畫融資，以及全球投資人會要求多少補償以承擔財政裁量權風險。美國殖利率曲線小幅趨陡，但跌勢範圍廣泛，顯示衝擊並非僅限於期限溢價。

信用及跨資產表現削弱了風險普遍撤退的論點。大盤僅小幅下跌，半導體股反彈、波動率下降，高收益債基金則近乎持平。現券信用市場的表現遠為不一。隨AI投資加劇資產負債表疑慮，Oracle利差擴大；市場對Amazon大型交易反應冷淡的影響延續至新的資料中心融資管線；而成本高昂的私募信貸交易則顯示，資本仍可取得，但僅限於發行人特定條件。

因此形成的論點是限制性的存續期間重新定價，而非融資失序。石油及成品油稀缺重啟通膨傳導管道；鷹派政策公信力與更高的市場借款成本推升實質殖利率；英國財政不確定性則加重全球期限溢價負擔。反向力量在於，市場仍大致排除聯準會近期行動，風險資產亦未證實存在廣泛清算。因此，疲弱數據仍可能恢復存續期間買盤，但舉證責任已轉向必須證明能源壓力會在傳導至工資、需求與政策之前消退。

## 美國公債利率、聯準會定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構

美國公債附息債殖利率全線上升，以殖利率曲線中段與長端領跌。2年期收於4.21%，上升3個基點；5年期上升5個基點至4.33%；10年期上升5個基點至4.60%；30年期則攀升5個基點至5.11%。此一排序逆轉週五的扭轉趨平。對政策敏感的債券價格下跌，但2年期以外更大的變動顯示，市場在本交易日也要求更多補償，以承擔通膨持續、全球財政供給，以及存續期間所需實質報酬的風險。

週線及月線期間進一步強化這項解讀。2年、5年及10年期殖利率1週內仍分別下跌5、4及2個基點，保留了6月溫和通膨數據所帶來的部分紓解。30年期則上升1個基點。以1個月計算，各天期變動分別為+2、+10、+14及+21個基點。近期政策重新定價曾雙向移動，但長端累積負擔持續增加。

殖利率曲線指標呈現受控的熊市趨陡。2s10s斜率擴大2個基點至39，2s30s亦擴大2個基點至90。2s5s10s蝶式利差上升2個基點至-15，2s10s30s也上升2個基點至-12。5年與10年期區段跌幅超過2年期，長債亦未如週五般表現較佳。這種型態符合市場同時提高預期實質利率路徑，以及在能源與財政不確定性下持有存續期間所需的補償。

抗通膨美國公債顯示，名目殖利率的調整多數來自實質殖利率。10年期實質殖利率上升4個基點至2.35%，10年期損益兩平通膨率則上升1個基點至2.25%。5年及7年期實質殖利率各上升4個基點，分別至2.05%及2.19%；20年期上升3個基點至2.70%，30年期則上升4個基點至2.91%。實質存續期間各處均出現廣泛漲幅。通膨補償對油價有所反應，但實質殖利率更大的變動顯示折現率提高，政策分布亦更偏緊。

能源傳導機制兼具地緣政治與實體層面。週一交易時段，伊朗支持的青年運動威脅封鎖來自沙烏地阿拉伯的海運，使曾協助繞過受阻荷莫茲海峽航運的紅海路線承壓。沙烏地阿拉伯上月經由該路線的出口量達每日419萬桶，創歷史新高。2條通道同時面臨威脅，提高運輸長期受阻的風險，並可能經由燃料供應、航運成本及庫存的預防性需求傳導至通膨。

成品油使市場更難將該風險視為單純波動劇烈的原油價格走勢。3-2-1裂解價差接近多年高點$70，超過歷史平均值6倍；美國平均汽油價格為每加侖$4.60，高於1年前的$3.63。WTI收漲0.8971%至$83.23，Brent上漲1.2713%至$89.22。原油供給與煉油產能錯配，意味單靠原油價格下跌，未必能為加油站價格帶來同等程度的緩解，因而延長其可能傳導至消費與通膨預期的時間。

聯準會政策定價仍集中在較後期。6月通膨報告轉弱後，交易員大致排除了7月底會議升息25個基點的可能性，但市場仍計入年底前收緊0.34個百分點，並認為9月及10月採取行動的機率具有實質意義。主席Kevin Warsh堅稱，單次溫和CPI數據並不代表抗通膨任務已經完成，為這項機率分布提供了制度性支持。因此，週一的賣壓恢復了今年稍後採取行動的選擇性，但未使下次會議升息成為基準情境。

另有1項具可信度的競爭性解讀。DoubleLine的Bill Campbell認為，居高不下的美國公債殖利率已在收緊借款條件，若通膨持續放緩，聯準會便可維持利率不變。他也認為，石油供給衝擊反而是政策保持耐心的理由，因為升息無法恢復流失的能源供給。市場收盤部分接受了這套邏輯：前端變動小於殖利率曲線中段及長端。問題在於，燃料成本若持續高企，仍可能改變預期與需求，即使立即升息無法有效補救，也會迫使限制性條件維持更久。

國庫券殖利率曲線顯示市場偏好近期流動性與再投資彈性，而非突然出現現金緊縮。4週及6週期票息等值殖利率均為3.70%，8週期殖利率為3.71%；單日變動分別為-1、0及-1個基點。13週、17週、26週及52週期殖利率收於3.82%、3.87%、3.97%及4.03%，分別上升2、2、5及3個基點。管理超過$8兆的貨幣市場基金將資金集中於數週內到期、展期或重設利率的證券，符合在政策路徑仍不明朗時保留選擇性的做法。

整體利率圖像同時反映下次會議風險偏低與存續期間門檻偏高。極短天期國庫券持穩，加上2年期變動較小，使即時融資與7月政策壓力仍受控制。各天期實質殖利率上升、殖利率曲線趨陡及較長天期國庫券殖利率走堅，顯示投資人仍要求補償，以承擔限制性環境可能延長的風險。石油提高通膨降溫停滯的機率；英國公債則顯示財政不確定性可以多快推升全球主權債殖利率。美國公債若要重啟漲勢，如今有賴證據顯示其中1項或2項壓力正在減弱。

## 信用、融資與初級市場承接

整體信用市場表現有序，但依存續期間而分化。隨美國公債殖利率上升，LQD下跌0.3812%至$107.15；HYG上漲0.0377%至$79.68；JNK則下跌0.0313%至$95.95。高收益債基金近乎持平且波動率下降，不支持風險資本普遍撤出的論點。投資級債總報酬承受較高的利率敏感度，而個別發行人現券定價則顯示，例行再融資、舉債擴張與資產負債表已接近評等界線者之間的差異遠為明顯。

Oracle是現券信用市場惡化最明顯的案例。其5年期違約保護年成本上升至約2.03個百分點，創可追溯至2008年底以來最高。其2056年到期、票息6.7%的債券利差擴大約8個基點至263，2036年到期、票息5.7%的票券則擴大約9個基點至205。S&P先前已將Oracle調降至僅高於非投資級1個級距；Moody’s維持Baa2評等，展望為負向。週一的利差擴大，使先前源於評等事件的疑慮進一步演變為次級市場持續承壓。

其機制在於資本支出、變現時程及龐大指數權重三者的碰撞。Oracle是美國高評級公司債指數中最大的非金融發行人，使其債務成為AI融資風險的指標。股權融資或客戶預付款可延後墮落天使壓力，但中期結果取決於執行力及現金創造能力。在第2家評等機構正式取消其投資級地位之前，投資人已計入更高的損失及流動性溢價，顯示買方基礎轉換可在正式遷移前很久就影響價值。

AI供給仍是活躍的技術面逆風。大型科技公司5年內將債務規模增加1倍後，投資人對Amazon的$250億債券發售反應冷淡。BlackRock正準備透過控股公司發行超過$120億債券，為其持有El Paso1項Meta資料中心計畫的80%股權融資；Meta持有其餘20%。這項架構將融資移至專案載體，但在現有產業估值承壓之際，仍要求固定收益投資組合承接另一大批科技相關存續期間。

Netflix提供了較單純的再融資案例，規模較小且有明確讓利水準。該公司推銷$10億、2036年到期票券，初步價格討論為美國公債殖利率加95個基點，所得資金擬用於償還約$10億於2026年稍後到期的債務，並支付公司費用。其2056年到期債券週一交易價格接近每百元面額92.94美分，為1年來最低盤中價。先前一次投資級債券發售獲得超過發行規模10倍的需求；本次執行則必須面對成長放緩及更擁擠的高評級債發行日程。

私募信貸顯示，即使融資成本極高，交易仍可完成。Shapoorji Pallonji Group完成1筆1,510億盧比、即$16億的交易，為Goswami Infratech的債務再融資。3年期零息盧比債券定價殖利率為18.95%，另有1筆3年期美元債券以14.5%籌得$6.5億。借款以Afcons Infrastructure及Tata Sons股份作為擔保，其中1,350億盧比須在24個月內償還。多元的本地與全球買方提供了流動性，但能否取得資金，主要取決於擔保品、殖利率及攤還安排。

巴西Oncoclinicas在困境程度上更進一步。這家醫療服務商在收購活動使其於本地2位數利率環境下提高槓桿後，已與部分債權人團體達成協議，將51億雷亞爾債務進行庭外重組。IG4尋求取得超過半數表決權，並提議透過優先於其他債務的可轉換債券，投入約5億雷亞爾新資本。該提案仍不具約束力，並以排他性與盡職調查為前提，因此提議的優先順位仍須與債權人協商，後者的回收順位可能遭到稀釋。

因此，初級及次級市場證據均指向選擇性承接。例行再融資仍可進入高評級債市場，大型AI相關專案也仍能利用深厚的融資管線，私募貸款機構則完成了跨幣別槓桿交易。價格、擔保品及結構性優先順位決定了資金取得條件。即使掛牌高收益債基金保持穩定，Oracle現券利差與違約保護仍顯示真實的信用惡化。固定收益市場的負擔集中於支出增速超過近期現金創造能力的發行人，或新資本要求對已承壓資產取得更優先求償權的發行人。

## 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導

英國提供了最強的跨境存續期間衝擊。10年期英國公債殖利率收盤上升8個基點至5.03%，30年期殖利率上升9個基點至5.75%，創5月20日以來最高。首相Andy Burnham表示其政府將運用現行財政規則內可用的彈性後，英鎊兌美元曾下跌0.3%。美國公債隨英國公債同步下跌，透過全球期限溢價及長天期主權債所需相對補償，傳導英國市場的重新定價。

市場疑慮集中於融資架構，而非政府已宣布放棄規則。Burnham承諾說明支出的資金來源，但擴大公共金融機構的提案，帶來更多舉債可能游離於主要債務限制之外的前景。Resolution Foundation呼籲向National Wealth Fund注資£160億，以吸引超過£400億的私人共同投資進入能源與基礎建設。機構分類可以改變規則的處理方式，但債券仍需要買方，相關利息也仍留在公共部門資產負債表上。

債務服務成本使這項差異具有實質經濟約束力。Resolution估計，每增加£10億借款，每年會增加約£5,000萬利息支出。英國借款成本已是10國集團最高，公共債務規模又約等同於1年經濟產出，因此投資人將財政彈性視為未來存續期間供給及利息負擔增加。英鎊下跌進一步強化了這項解讀：更積極的財政企圖並未帶來有利成長的匯率反應，使通膨與海外需求成為英國公債殖利率曲線的額外限制。

歐元區企業借款條件呈現另一種形式的限制。在ECB第2季調查中，淨42%的企業回報銀行貸款利率上升，高於第1季的26%。另分別有淨31%及10%的企業回報其他融資成本及擔保品要求提高。整體貸款可得性大致穩定，但大型企業淨改善4%，中小企業則淨惡化4%。融資缺口由2%擴大至3%。

這項分化能在無須出現全面信用緊縮的情況下，將政策利率傳導至投資。淨29%的企業將經濟前景列為取得外部融資的限制。在計畫投資AI的公司中，72%預期仰賴內部資金，約各16%提及銀行貸款、補助或租賃，僅1%預期以債務證券作為資金來源。因此，即使整體貸款可得性看似穩定，更高的借款成本仍可能抑制資本支出的速度並改變其融資組合。

美國及歐洲訊號分別強化了同一場存續期間辯論的不同面向。歐元區企業融資條件收緊，會對未來成長構成拖累並支撐德國公債存續期間；英國財政擴張則提高英國公債的供給與通膨門檻。週一美國公債賣壓吸收了更多英國因素，因其影響即時且已由市場定價。歐元區調查的影響較慢，但強化了高市場利率可以代替央行完成部分工作的論點。全球存續期間仍取決於主權借款需求與民間部門緊縮之間的平衡。

## 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險

美國實質殖利率上升及美元走強，為新興市場債務帶來溫和的共同逆風。EMB下跌0.1675%至$95.37，美元指數則上升0.2382%至100.99。基金走勢顯示的是溫和總報酬回落，而非主權債利差一致受到衝擊。當地政策公信力、大宗商品曝險及國內買方深度仍是決定因素，尤其是央行已運用高利率捍衛本幣的市場。

印尼展現了這項取捨。印尼盾已從兌美元18,190的歷史低點回升約1.3%，至前次收盤17,895；3個月隱含波動率則由7.93%降至6.78%。印尼央行在2026年累計升息100個基點，並收緊投機性資金流動規定。這些措施支撐了匯率穩定及印尼盾資產的吸引力，但也維持了偏高的國內融資門檻。

主權信用及債券收益提供了支撐。S&P於7月13日維持印尼的投資級評等與展望，指標10年期政府債券殖利率約為7.25%。外國投資人可望連續第2個月淨買入。這項支撐仰賴政策：分析師預期財政赤字維持在GDP的3%以下，而市場對政府與央行協調的信心仍是外資需求的核心。

石油會透過補貼成本及經常帳，使穩定進程變得複雜。能源價格上漲會提高保護家庭免受衝擊的財政成本，減少政府在央行捍衛匯率之際支撐經濟活動的空間。經常帳赤字也限制了美國政策前景轉向較不鷹派能將印尼盾推升多遠。印尼的高殖利率可以吸引資金流入，但該殖利率也反映抵銷外部壓力所需的國內政策力度。週一美元與實質利率的變動提高了這項外部門檻，但並未抹去波動率已取得的改善。

新興市場的結論是差異化，而非全面防禦。美國公債實質殖利率上升及美元走強削弱了共同環境，但印尼仍保有投資級支撐、外資參與，以及願意捍衛匯率的央行。弱點在於，若油價推升補貼與財政負擔，維持這項防衛的成本將提高。債務管理公信力可維持本地債券的吸引力；若協調失靈，影響將迅速傳導至印尼盾、本地殖利率及外國買方基礎。

## 跨資產

美國股市未證實債券賣壓代表廣泛避險。S&P 500下跌0.1932%至7,443.2798，Nasdaq 100則上漲0.0405%至28,604.2305，費城半導體指數上升0.5993%至11,743.8496。VIX下跌0.6393%至18.65。多數股票走弱，但半導體股反彈及波動率下降顯示，投資人並未在美國公債殖利率上升時不加區別地清算風險資產。

較長期間的表現使AI疑慮持續存在。半導體指數1週內仍下跌4.891%，1個月內下跌18.1144%；Nasdaq 100月線則下跌5.9263%。單日反彈未能消除市場對AI投資收入能否及時實現，以支撐股票估值及債務累積的疑問。同樣的不確定性也推升Oracle現券利差，並使每一筆新的科技相關融資都成為產業集中度的考驗。

大宗商品及匯率走勢與利率變動更為一致。紅海威脅使面臨風險的出口航線增加，原油因而上漲，美元亦走強。儘管衝突再起，黃金期貨仍下跌0.0598%至$4,010.30。美元走強及實質殖利率升高壓過傳統避險反應，進一步支持本交易日的核心是通膨與政策限制，而非恐慌驅動的防禦性資產需求。

跨資產組合也包含1項重要限制。大盤僅溫和下跌、高收益債基金持穩且波動率下降，因此實質殖利率上升尚未透過風險資產形成普遍緊縮。能源是共同的不利衝擊，串聯消費者成本、聯準會定價及主權國家的進口負擔。若這項傳導持續，而股市波動率仍受控制，固定收益市場可能繼續先透過實質殖利率及殖利率曲線承擔大部分調整，之後信用風險才擴散至受影響發行人以外。

## 前瞻市場意義

7月23日下午1:00（ET）標售的$210億新發10年期TIPS，將於7月31日交割、2036年7月15日到期；其後為7月24日上午10:00（ET）公布的6月新屋銷售。兩者將區分實質存續期間承接與利率敏感型需求。發售後次級市場實質殖利率持穩且住房需求疲弱，將證實週一的債券價格下跌已提供足夠補償，同時限制性借款成本正在抑制經濟活動。實質殖利率持續走弱而新屋銷售仍具韌性，則會同時形成更高供給承接門檻及更強需求，因而挑戰這項解讀。10年期實質殖利率、損益兩平通膨率、2s10s殖利率曲線及房貸敏感型信用，是目前石油與實質利率論點將獲得確認或重新受到質疑的傳導管道。

7月21日上午4:00（ET）的歐元區銀行放款調查，以及7月24日上午2:00（ET）的6月英國零售銷售，將釐清高借款成本是否帶來足夠的民間部門緊縮，以抵銷歐洲的通膨及財政壓力。核准標準收緊、貸款需求轉弱及英國消費疲軟，將確認ECB企業調查傳達的訊息，並為德國公債與英國公債前端提供成長面的反向力量，以抗衡週一的財政賣壓。標準持穩及支出具韌性將削弱這項抵銷效果，尤其是在長天期英國公債殖利率仍居高不下時。7月23日下午7:30（ET）公布的日本6月全國CPI，則增添全球殖利率邊界：通膨持續將提高JGB門檻，並重新引發對海外存續期間的競爭；明確降溫則會緩解壓力。

華盛頓與AI產業的信用承接有一套明確時序。哥倫比亞特區的$12億所得稅擔保債券，預計在Moody’s將其Aa1評等展望穩定後於7月21日定價；Netflix最終條件，以及BlackRock計畫為Meta相關El Paso資料中心安排超過$120億融資，則將顯示不同借款人需要多少讓利。訂單深厚、讓利受控及次級市場交易穩定，將確認即使美國公債殖利率上升，資金仍然可得。後續表現疲弱、價格大幅修正，或現有AI相關債券利差再次擴大，則會挑戰這項解讀，並重啟集中式資本支出正耗盡高評級債需求的風險。市政債讓利、Oracle殖利率曲線及更廣泛的投資級債利差，是敏感的傳導點。
zh-TW.methodology_note: 除非另有註明，市場水準均以紐約收盤為基準。

overall_summary.headline: 能源與財政風險推升實質殖利率，美國公債呈熊市趨陡
overall_summary.session_narrative: 美國公債全線走跌，以殖利率曲線中段與長端領跌，形成受控的熊市趨陡，名目殖利率升幅主要由實質殖利率貢獻。沙烏地阿拉伯出口航線面臨威脅、煉油產能吃緊，以及英國公債大幅重新定價，將能源與財政壓力傳導至美國存續期間溢價。前端變動較小，高收益債基金近乎持平，波動率亦下降，削弱了聯準會即將採取行動或市場普遍清算的論點。
overall_summary.summary: 紐約收盤逆轉先前的實質存續期間漲勢，石油供應中斷風險與英國財政不確定性提高了持有存續期間的門檻。名目殖利率多數上漲來自實質殖利率，而高收益債基金持穩及波動率下降，使這波走勢尚未演變為廣泛的融資衝擊。

section_summaries.market_read: 利率衝擊仍屬存續期間重新定價，因風險資產並未證實市場正在清算。
section_summaries.next_check: 尚待確認的關鍵是能源壓力是否會傳導至需求、通膨預期與政策。
section_summaries.summary: 石油航線威脅與煉油產能稀缺重啟通膨傳導管道，英國財政不確定性則透過全球期限溢價增添壓力。美國公債跌勢由實質殖利率主導，但股市僅溫和走弱、波動率下降及高收益債基金持穩，使收盤走勢仍符合限制性的存續期間重新定價，而非融資失序。

section_summaries.market_read: 收盤提高了實質利率與期限溢價門檻，但未將近期升息推至核心情境。
section_summaries.next_check: 隨能源與財政壓力演變，觀察實質殖利率上升與殖利率曲線趨陡是否延續。
section_summaries.section_title: 美國公債利率、聯準會定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構
section_summaries.summary: 附息債殖利率普遍上升，殖利率曲線中段與長端表現落後前端，曲線呈熊市趨陡。名目利率調整多由實質殖利率帶動，而較長天期國庫券殖利率走堅、極短天期國庫券持穩，顯示壓力集中於限制性環境延續及存續期間補償，而非即時融資壓力。

section_summaries.market_read: 基金表現有序，掩蓋了支出增速超過現金創造能力之發行人的個別壓力。
section_summaries.next_check: 新券訂單、讓利與次級市場交易將檢驗信用需求的深度。
section_summaries.section_title: 信用、融資與初級市場承接
section_summaries.summary: 信用市場仍保持開放，但選擇性極高：對利率敏感的投資級債走弱，高收益債基金則近乎持平。Oracle利差擴大顯示AI支出與評等風險周邊狀況惡化，而新的科技及私募信貸融資仍僅能透過借款人特定的讓利、擔保品或優先順位完成。

section_summaries.market_read: 全球存續期間目前在主權債供給壓力與高利率造成的成長抑制之間取得平衡。
section_summaries.next_check: 英國消費與歐洲貸款需求必須顯示民間部門緊縮是否正在加深。
section_summaries.section_title: 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導
section_summaries.summary: 英國財政彈性推升英國公債殖利率，並將更高的主權債供給與期限溢價負擔傳導至美國公債。同時，歐元區企業回報融資成本更高且分化不均，意味民間部門緊縮唯有超過財政舉債疑慮更迅速的市場影響，才能對存續期間形成支撐。

section_summaries.market_read: 新興市場存續期間仍呈差異化，國內政策公信力抵銷了更嚴峻的美元環境。
section_summaries.next_check: 觀察石油成本是否削弱印尼的財政協調、匯率或外資需求。
section_summaries.section_title: 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險
section_summaries.summary: 美國實質殖利率上升及美元走強形成溫和的共同逆風，但印尼的政策防衛、投資級支撐與外資流入維持了差異化表現。石油仍是主要弱點，因補貼成本可能壓縮財政空間，並提高維持匯率穩定的代價。

section_summaries.market_read: 大部分調整由固定收益市場承擔，整體風險偏好仍然完整。
section_summaries.next_check: 股市波動率上升或信用市場更廣泛走弱，將顯示壓力已由利率向外傳導。
section_summaries.summary: 股市僅小幅下跌，半導體股反彈且波動率下降，未對美國公債賣壓形成廣泛避險確認。石油與美元走勢呼應實質殖利率上升，黃金則走軟，顯示跨資產訊號由通膨與政策限制主導，而非避險需求。

section_summaries.market_read: 下一步確認需要存續期間、經濟活動與信用承接同時提供證據。
section_summaries.next_check: 追蹤TIPS發售、歐洲放款、英國零售銷售、日本CPI及新券後續表現。
section_summaries.summary: 3項檢驗將決定重新定價能否持續：實質存續期間承接相對於住房需求、歐洲與日本的成長通膨證據相對於財政壓力，以及市場對市政債與AI相關供給的信用需求。實質殖利率穩定、經濟活動轉弱及發債有序將支持現有解讀；反向結果則會使其重新受到質疑。

long_thesis.english_markdown:
## Opening Fixed-Income Thesis

The July 20 close reversed Friday’s real-duration rally and converted its energy constraint into an active rates repricing. The 10-year Treasury yield rose 5 basis points to 4.60%, with 4 basis points of the move coming through a 2.35% real yield. WTI’s rise to $83.23 kept the inflation tail alive, yet the dominance of real-yield cheapening showed that investors were also raising the hurdle for holding duration. The shift was consequential: patience at the next Federal Reserve meeting remained plausible, but confidence in a benign later-year path weakened.

Energy supplied the clearest catalyst. Threats to Saudi exports through the Red Sea put a second route at risk while traffic through the Strait of Hormuz remained heavily impaired. Tight refining capacity made the transmission broader than crude alone, leaving gasoline and diesel costs capable of reaching consumers, freight and inflation expectations even without a return to the year’s crude-price peak. That prospect preserved later-year tightening risk and made the prior close’s combination of lower real yields and higher breakevens difficult to sustain.

Global fiscal duration added a separate source of pressure. UK gilts sold off sharply after new Prime Minister Andy Burnham discussed using flexibility within existing fiscal rules for investment. Treasuries tracked part of that move, linking the US long end to a renewed debate over how heavily indebted sovereigns finance growth plans and how much compensation global investors require for fiscal discretion. The US curve steepened modestly, though losses were broad enough to show that the shock extended beyond term premium alone.

Credit and cross assets limited the case for a generalized retreat from risk. Broad equities slipped only slightly, semiconductor shares rebounded, volatility declined and high-yield funds were nearly flat. Cash credit was much less uniform. Oracle’s spreads widened as AI investment intensified balance-sheet concerns, subdued reception for a large Amazon deal carried into a fresh data-center financing pipeline, and expensive private-credit transactions showed that capital remained available only at issuer-specific terms.

The resulting thesis is one of restrictive duration repricing rather than funding disorder. Oil and refined-product scarcity reopened the inflation channel; hawkish policy credibility and higher market borrowing costs moved real yields; UK fiscal uncertainty reinforced the global term-premium burden. The counterforce is that near-term Fed action was still largely discounted and risk assets did not validate a broad liquidation. Softer data could therefore restore a duration bid, but the burden of evidence has shifted toward showing that energy pressure will fade before it reaches wages, demand and policy.

## UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure

Treasury yields rose across the coupon curve, with the belly and long end leading the daily loss. The 2-year finished at 4.21%, up 3 basis points; the 5-year rose 5 basis points to 4.33%; the 10-year increased 5 to 4.60%; and the 30-year climbed 5 to 5.11%. The ordering reversed Friday’s twist flattening. Policy-sensitive paper cheapened, yet the larger move beyond 2 years showed that the session also required more compensation for persistent inflation, global fiscal supply and the real return demanded from duration.

The weekly and monthly horizons sharpen that interpretation. 2-, 5- and 10-year yields remained 5, 4 and 2 basis points lower over 1 week, preserving some of the relief generated by benign June inflation data. The 30-year was 1 basis point higher. Over 1 month, the respective changes were +2, +10, +14 and +21 basis points. Near-term policy repricing had moved in both directions, whereas the accumulated long-end burden continued to build.

Curve measures recorded a controlled bear steepening. The 2s10s slope widened 2 basis points to 39, and 2s30s widened 2 to 90. The 2s5s10s butterfly rose 2 basis points to -15, while 2s10s30s also rose 2 to -12. The 5- and 10-year sectors lost more ground than the 2-year, and the long bond did not outperform as it had on Friday. That pattern fits a market raising both the expected real-rate path and compensation for holding duration through energy and fiscal uncertainty.

Inflation-linked Treasuries placed most of the nominal adjustment in real yields. The 10-year real yield rose 4 basis points to 2.35%, while the 10-year breakeven increased 1 basis point to 2.25%. 5- and 7-year real yields each rose 4 basis points, to 2.05% and 2.19%; the 20-year increased 3 to 2.70%, and the 30-year rose 4 to 2.91%. The rise was broad across real duration. Inflation compensation responded to oil, but the larger real move indicated a higher discount rate and a firmer policy distribution.

The energy mechanism had both geopolitical and physical components. During Monday’s session, the Iran-backed Houthis threatened to block maritime traffic from Saudi Arabia, placing the Red Sea route under pressure after it had helped bypass impaired Hormuz flows. Saudi exports through that route reached a record 4.19 million barrels a day in the prior month. A threat to both corridors raises the risk of persistent transport disruption, which can reach inflation through fuel availability, shipping costs and the precautionary demand for inventories.

Refined products made that risk harder to dismiss as a volatile crude move. The 3-2-1 crack spread stood near a multi-year high of $70, more than 6 times its historical average, and average US gasoline was $4.60 a gallon versus $3.63 a year earlier. WTI closed 0.8971% higher at $83.23 and Brent gained 1.2713% to $89.22. The mismatch between crude supply and refining capacity means lower crude alone may not deliver equivalent relief at the pump, extending the possible pass-through into consumption and inflation expectations.

Fed pricing remained back-loaded. Traders had largely removed a quarter-point increase at the end-of-July meeting after the softer June inflation reports, yet markets carried 0.34 percentage point of tightening by year-end and meaningful odds of action in September and October. Chair Kevin Warsh’s insistence that one benign CPI reading did not complete the inflation task gave that distribution institutional support. Monday’s selloff therefore restored later-year optionality without turning the next meeting into the base case.

There is a credible competing interpretation. Bill Campbell of DoubleLine argued that elevated Treasury yields are already tightening borrowing conditions and could allow the Fed to remain on hold if inflation continues to moderate. He also viewed an oil supply shock as a reason for policy patience because higher rates cannot restore lost energy supply. The market close partly accepted that logic: the front end moved less than the belly and long end. The challenge is that sustained fuel costs can still alter expectations and demand, requiring restrictive conditions to last longer even when an immediate hike offers little remedy.

The bill curve showed a preference for near-term liquidity and reinvestment flexibility, not an abrupt cash squeeze. 4- and 6-week coupon-equivalent yields were 3.70%, and the 8-week yield was 3.71%; daily changes were -1, zero and -1 basis point. 13-, 17-, 26- and 52-week yields closed at 3.82%, 3.87%, 3.97% and 4.03%, rising 2, 2, 5 and 3 basis points. Money-market funds overseeing more than $8 trillion were concentrating in securities that mature, roll or reset within weeks, consistent with preserving optionality while the policy path remained unsettled.

The combined rates picture reconciles a small near-meeting risk with a demanding duration hurdle. Stable very short bills and the smaller 2-year move left immediate funding and July policy stress contained. Higher real yields across maturities, a steeper curve and firmer longer bills showed that investors still required compensation for the possibility of prolonged restriction. Oil raised the chance that disinflation stalls; gilts demonstrated how quickly fiscal uncertainty can lift global sovereign yields. A renewed Treasury rally now depends on evidence that one or both pressures are losing force.

## Credit, Funding, And Primary-Market Absorption

Broad credit performance was orderly but divided by duration. LQD fell 0.3812% to $107.15 as Treasury yields rose, while HYG gained 0.0377% to $79.68 and JNK slipped 0.0313% to $95.95. The near-flat high-yield funds and lower volatility argued against a generalized withdrawal of risk capital. Investment-grade total returns carried more rate sensitivity, and issuer-level cash pricing revealed a much sharper distinction between routine refinancing, debt-funded expansion and balance sheets already close to a ratings boundary.

Oracle marked the clearest deterioration in cash credit. Its 5-year default-protection cost rose to about 2.03 percentage points annually, the highest in a history extending to late 2008. The spread on its 6.7% bond due 2056 widened about 8 basis points to 263, and the 5.7% note due 2036 widened roughly 9 to 205. S&P had already lowered Oracle to one notch above speculative grade; Moody’s held a Baa2 rating with a negative outlook. Monday’s widening deepened the prior concern from a rating event into continuing secondary-market pressure.

The mechanism is the collision between capital expenditure, monetization timing and a large index footprint. Oracle is the biggest nonfinancial issuer in the US high-grade corporate-bond index, making its debt a barometer for AI financing risk. Equity or customer prepayments can delay fallen-angel pressure, but the medium-term outcome depends on execution and cash generation. Investors were applying a wider loss and liquidity premium before any second rating agency removed investment-grade status, showing that the buyer-base transition can affect value well ahead of a formal migration.

AI supply remained an active technical headwind. Investor response to Amazon’s $25 billion bond sale was subdued after Big Tech doubled its debt load over 5 years. BlackRock was preparing more than $12 billion of bonds through the holding company for its 80% stake in a Meta data-center project in El Paso; Meta owns the remaining 20%. The structure shifts financing to a project vehicle, but it still asks fixed-income portfolios to absorb another large block of technology-linked duration as existing sector valuations are under pressure.

Netflix offered a cleaner refinancing case with less scale and a visible concession point. The company marketed $1 billion of notes due 2036 at initial talk of 95 basis points over Treasuries, with proceeds intended to repay roughly $1 billion maturing later in 2026 and cover corporate expenses. Its 2056 bonds traded near 92.94 cents on Monday, their lowest intraday price in a year. A previous investment-grade sale drew demand exceeding 10 times its size; current execution must contend with slowing growth and a more crowded high-grade calendar.

Private credit demonstrated that completion could coexist with a very high funding cost. Shapoorji Pallonji Group completed a 151 billion-rupee, or $1.6 billion, transaction to refinance debt at Goswami Infratech. The 3-year zero-coupon rupee bonds were priced to yield 18.95%, and a separate 3-year dollar bond raised $650 million at 14.5%. The borrowings were backed by shares in Afcons Infrastructure and Tata Sons, with 135 billion rupees required to be repaid within 24 months. Diverse local and global buyers provided liquidity, but collateral, yield and amortization carried the burden of access.

Brazil’s Oncoclinicas sat farther along the distress continuum. The medical provider had agreed with part of its creditor group on an out-of-court restructuring of 5.1 billion reais of debt after acquisitions increased leverage under double-digit local rates. IG4 sought more than half of the voting rights and proposed about 500 million reais of fresh capital through convertible bonds with priority over other debt. The offer remained nonbinding and contingent on exclusivity and due diligence, so the proposed seniority must still be negotiated with creditors whose recovery position could be diluted.

Primary and secondary evidence therefore pointed to selective absorption. Routine refinancing could reach the high-grade market, large AI-linked projects still had access to a deep pipeline, and private lenders completed leveraged deals across currencies. Price, collateral and structural priority determined that access. Oracle’s cash spreads and default protection showed genuine credit deterioration even as listed high-yield funds stayed stable. The fixed-income burden was concentrated in issuers where spending outran near-term cash generation or where new capital required a superior claim on already-stressed assets.

## Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission

The UK supplied the strongest cross-border duration impulse. 10-year gilt yields closed 8 basis points higher at 5.03%, and 30-year yields rose 9 to 5.75%, their highest since May 20. Sterling fell as much as 0.3% against the dollar after Prime Minister Andy Burnham said his government would use available flexibility within existing fiscal rules. Treasuries fell alongside gilts, transmitting the UK repricing through global term premium and the relative compensation required from long sovereign bonds.

The concern centered on financing architecture, not an announced abandonment of the rules. Burnham pledged to show how spending would be funded, but proposals to expand public financial institutions raised the prospect of more borrowing outside the main debt constraint. The Resolution Foundation called for a £16 billion injection into the National Wealth Fund to attract more than £40 billion of private co-investment in energy and infrastructure. Institutional classification can alter the rule treatment, yet the bonds still require buyers and the associated interest remains on the public balance sheet.

Debt service makes that distinction economically binding. Resolution estimated that each additional £1 billion of borrowing adds about £50 million of annual interest expense. With UK borrowing costs already the highest in the Group of 10 and public debt around the size of annual output, investors treated fiscal flexibility as an increase in future duration supply and interest burden. The pound’s decline reinforced the interpretation: easier fiscal ambition did not generate a growth-positive currency response, leaving inflation and overseas demand as additional constraints on the gilt curve.

Euro-area corporate borrowing conditions supplied a different form of restriction. In the ECB’s Q2 survey, a net 42% of firms reported higher bank-loan interest rates, up from 26% in Q1. Net shares of 31% and 10% reported higher other financing costs and collateral requirements. Aggregate loan availability was broadly steady, yet it improved by a net 4% for large firms and deteriorated by a net 4% for small and medium-sized companies. The financing gap widened to 3% from 2%.

That dispersion connects policy rates to investment without requiring an outright credit contraction. A net 29% of firms cited the economic outlook as a constraint on external finance. Among companies planning AI investment, 72% expected to rely on internal funds, around 16% each named bank loans, grants or leasing, and only 1% expected debt securities to be the source. Higher borrowing costs can therefore restrain the pace and financing mix of capital expenditure even when headline loan availability appears stable.

The US and European signals reinforce different sides of the same duration debate. Tighter euro-area corporate terms create a future growth drag and can support Bund duration, whereas UK fiscal expansion raises the supply and inflation hurdle for gilts. Monday’s Treasury selloff absorbed more of the UK channel because it was immediate and market-priced. The euro survey is slower-moving, but it strengthens the argument that high market rates can perform part of central banks’ work. Global duration remains exposed to the balance between sovereign borrowing demand and private-sector restraint.

## EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk

Emerging-market debt faced a modest common headwind from higher US real yields and a firmer dollar. EMB fell 0.1675% to $95.37, while the Dollar Index rose 0.2382% to 100.99. The fund move described a mild total-return setback rather than a uniform sovereign-spread shock. Local policy credibility, commodity exposure and the depth of domestic buyers remained decisive, especially where central banks were already using high rates to defend currencies.

Indonesia illustrated that trade-off. The rupiah had recovered about 1.3% from a record low of 18,190 per dollar to a prior close of 17,895, and 3-month implied volatility had fallen to 6.78% from 7.93%. Bank Indonesia had raised rates by a cumulative 100 basis points during 2026 and tightened rules on speculative flows. Those measures supported currency stability and the attractiveness of rupiah assets, but they also preserved a high domestic funding hurdle.

Sovereign credit and bond carry provided anchors. S&P kept Indonesia’s investment-grade rating and outlook on July 13, and the benchmark 10-year government bond offered about 7.25%. Foreign investors were on course for a 2nd consecutive month of net purchases. This support was policy-dependent: analysts expected the fiscal deficit to stay below 3% of GDP, and confidence in coordination between the government and central bank remained central to foreign demand.

Oil complicates that stabilization through subsidy costs and the current account. Higher energy prices increase the fiscal cost of cushioning households, reducing the room to support activity while the central bank protects the currency. A current-account deficit also limits how far a less hawkish US outlook can carry the rupiah. Indonesia’s high yield can attract inflows, yet the same yield reflects the domestic policy effort needed to offset external pressure. Monday’s dollar and real-rate move raised that external hurdle without erasing the improvement already achieved in volatility.

The EM conclusion is differentiated rather than uniformly defensive. Higher Treasury real yields and a stronger dollar weakened the common backdrop, but Indonesia retained an investment-grade anchor, foreign participation and a central bank willing to protect the currency. The vulnerability lies in the cost of maintaining that defense if oil raises subsidies and the fiscal burden. Debt-management credibility can keep local bonds attractive; a loss of coordination would transmit quickly through the rupiah, local yields and the foreign buyer base.

## Cross asset

US equities did not validate a broad risk-off interpretation of the bond selloff. The S&P 500 declined 0.1932% to 7,443.2798, whereas the Nasdaq 100 gained 0.0405% to 28,604.2305 and the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index rose 0.5993% to 11,743.8496. The VIX fell 0.6393% to 18.65. Most shares weakened, but the rebound in semiconductors and lower volatility showed that investors were not liquidating risk indiscriminately as Treasury yields rose.

The longer horizon kept the AI concern alive. The semiconductor index remained down 4.891% over 1 week and 18.1144% over 1 month, while the Nasdaq 100 was 5.9263% lower over the month. A 1-day bounce did not remove questions about whether the revenue from AI investment will arrive fast enough to justify equity valuations and debt accumulation. That same uncertainty widened Oracle’s cash spreads and made each new technology-linked financing a test of sector concentration.

Commodities and currencies aligned more closely with the rates move. Crude advanced as the Red Sea threat widened the set of vulnerable export routes, while the dollar strengthened. Gold futures slipped 0.0598% to $4,010.30 despite renewed conflict. A stronger dollar and higher real yields outweighed a conventional haven response, reinforcing the view that the session centered on inflation and policy restriction rather than fear-driven demand for defensive assets.

The cross-asset mix also contained an important limit. Broad equities were only modestly lower, high-yield funds were stable and volatility declined, so higher real yields had not yet produced a generalized tightening through risky assets. Energy was the common adverse shock, linking consumer costs, Fed pricing and sovereign import burdens. If that channel persists while equity volatility remains contained, fixed income may continue to bear most of the adjustment through real yields and curves before credit risk broadens beyond exposed issuers.

## Forward Market Significance

The $21 billion new 10-year TIPS auction at 1:00 p.m. ET on July 23, settling July 31 and maturing July 15, 2036, followed by June new residential sales at 10:00 a.m. ET on July 24, will separate real-duration absorption from rates-sensitive demand. Stable secondary real yields after the sale and soft home demand would confirm that Monday’s cheapening created enough compensation while restrictive borrowing costs are slowing activity. Persistent real-yield weakness alongside resilient home sales would challenge that reading by combining a higher supply hurdle with firmer demand. The 10-year real yield, breakeven inflation, the 2s10s curve and mortgage-sensitive credit are the channels through which the current oil-and-real-rate thesis would be confirmed or reopened.

The euro-area bank lending survey at 4:00 a.m. ET on July 21 and June UK retail sales at 2:00 a.m. ET on July 24 will clarify whether high borrowing costs are generating enough private-sector restraint to offset Europe’s inflation and fiscal pressures. Tighter approval standards, weaker loan demand and soft UK consumption would confirm the ECB firm survey’s message and give Bund and gilt front ends a growth counterweight to Monday’s fiscal selloff. Steady standards and resilient spending would challenge that offset, particularly if long gilt yields remain elevated. Japan’s June national CPI at 7:30 p.m. ET on July 23 adds the global-yield boundary: persistent inflation would raise the JGB hurdle and reopen competition for overseas duration, whereas a clear cooling would ease it.

Credit absorption has a direct sequence in Washington and the AI complex. The District of Columbia’s $1.2 billion income-tax-backed sale is expected to price July 21 after Moody’s stabilized its Aa1 outlook, while final Netflix terms and BlackRock’s planned financing of more than $12 billion for the Meta-linked El Paso data center will show how much concession different borrowers require. Deep orders, restrained concessions and stable secondary trading would confirm that cash remains available despite higher Treasury yields. Weak follow-through, material price revisions or renewed widening in existing AI-linked bonds would challenge that interpretation and reopen the risk that concentrated capital spending is exhausting high-grade demand. Municipal concessions, Oracle’s curve and broader investment-grade spreads are the sensitive transmission points.

section_summaries.market_read: The rates shock remained a duration repricing because risky assets did not confirm liquidation.
section_summaries.next_check: The unresolved test is whether energy pressure reaches demand, inflation expectations and policy.
section_summaries.summary: Oil-route threats and refining scarcity revived the inflation channel, while UK fiscal uncertainty added pressure through global term premium. Treasury losses were led by real yields, but subdued equity weakness, lower volatility and stable high-yield funds kept the close consistent with restrictive duration repricing rather than funding disorder.

section_summaries.market_read: The close raised the real-rate and term-premium hurdle without making a near-term hike central.
section_summaries.next_check: Watch whether real yields and curve steepening persist as energy and fiscal pressures evolve.
section_summaries.section_title: UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure
section_summaries.summary: Coupon yields rose broadly, with the belly and long end underperforming the front end as the curve bear-steepened. Real yields drove most of the nominal adjustment, while firm longer bills and stable very short bills located the pressure in prolonged restriction and duration compensation rather than immediate funding stress.

section_summaries.market_read: Orderly fund performance masks issuer-level stress where spending outruns cash generation.
section_summaries.next_check: New-issue orders, concessions and secondary trading will test the depth of credit demand.
section_summaries.summary: Credit remained open but highly selective: rate-sensitive investment grade weakened while high-yield funds stayed nearly flat. Oracle’s widening showed deterioration around AI spending and ratings risk, as new technology and private-credit financing still cleared only through borrower-specific concession, collateral or seniority.

section_summaries.market_read: Global duration now balances sovereign supply pressure against growth restraint from high rates.
section_summaries.next_check: UK consumption and European lending demand must show whether private restraint is deepening.
section_summaries.section_title: Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission
section_summaries.summary: UK fiscal flexibility lifted gilt yields and transmitted a higher sovereign-supply and term-premium burden into Treasuries. Euro-area firms simultaneously reported costlier, uneven financing, implying that private-sector restraint can support duration only if it outweighs the faster market impact of fiscal borrowing concerns.

section_summaries.market_read: EM duration remains differentiated, with domestic credibility offsetting a harsher dollar backdrop.
section_summaries.next_check: Watch whether oil costs weaken Indonesia’s fiscal coordination, currency or foreign demand.
section_summaries.section_title: EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk
section_summaries.summary: Higher US real yields and a firmer dollar created a mild common headwind, but Indonesia’s policy defense, investment-grade anchor and foreign inflows preserved differentiation. Oil remains the key vulnerability because subsidy costs could narrow fiscal room and raise the price of maintaining currency stability.

section_summaries.market_read: Fixed income bore most of the adjustment while broader risk appetite remained intact.
section_summaries.next_check: A rise in equity volatility or broader credit weakness would signal transmission beyond rates.
section_summaries.summary: Equities slipped only modestly, semiconductors rebounded and volatility declined, leaving no broad risk-off confirmation of the Treasury selloff. Oil and the dollar aligned with higher real yields, while gold softened, showing that inflation and policy restriction—not haven demand—dominated the cross-asset message.

section_summaries.market_read: The next confirmation requires simultaneous evidence from duration, activity and credit absorption.
section_summaries.next_check: Track the TIPS sale, European lending, UK retail sales, Japan CPI and new-issue follow-through.
section_summaries.summary: 3 tests will determine whether the repricing holds: real-duration absorption against housing demand, European and Japanese growth-inflation evidence against fiscal pressure, and credit demand for municipal and AI-linked supply. Stable real yields, softer activity and orderly issuance would support the interpretation; opposite outcomes would reopen it.

methodology_note: Market levels are shown on an NY-close basis unless otherwise noted.

bullets: 10Y TIPS real yield 2.35% (+4 bp) | 10Y breakeven inflation 2.25% (+1 bp)
UST 2s10s curve 39 bp (+2 bp) | UST 2s30s curve 90 bp (+2 bp)
overall_summary.headline: Treasuries bear-steepened as energy and fiscal risks lifted real yields
overall_summary.session_narrative: Treasuries sold off across the curve, with the belly and long end leading a controlled bear steepening and real yields supplying most of the nominal move. Threats to Saudi export routes, tight refining capacity and a sharp gilt repricing transmitted energy and fiscal pressure into the US duration premium. The front end moved less, while high-yield funds stayed nearly flat and volatility declined, limiting the case for imminent Fed action or generalized liquidation.
overall_summary.summary: The NY close reversed the prior real-duration rally as oil disruption risk and UK fiscal uncertainty raised the hurdle for holding duration. Most nominal cheapening came through real yields, while stable high-yield funds and lower volatility kept the move short of a broad funding shock.

watch_events.market_implication: The first supports duration; the second raises the supply hurdle and pressures mortgage credit.
watch_events.market_trigger: Stable real yields with soft sales, or persistent weakness alongside resilient demand.
watch_events.title: Real-duration absorption and housing demand
watch_events.watch_line: Compare post-auction real-yield stability with new-home demand, breakevens and the curve.

watch_events.market_implication: More restraint supports Bund and gilt front ends; resilience or firm CPI extends global yield pressure.
watch_events.market_trigger: Private-sector weakening versus resilient spending, with Japan inflation setting the yield boundary.
watch_events.title: Europe growth restraint and global yields
watch_events.watch_line: Test European lending and UK consumption against Japan inflation and long-gilt yields.

watch_events.market_implication: Orderly execution contains high-grade spreads; weak follow-through signals demand fatigue.
watch_events.market_trigger: Deep orders and stable follow-through versus price revisions or renewed AI-bond widening.
watch_events.title: Municipal and AI-linked credit absorption
watch_events.watch_line: Compare municipal concessions and AI-linked demand through secondary trading and Oracle’s curve.
